transcript:
Right now you're watching a swirl of gold-white threads tighten into a glowing storm — a small cluster of points tracing loops, leaving dotted trails behind them, until the whole thing blooms into a churning nebula ringed with those older paths.

Here's the part that got me: those glowing orbs aren't just orbiting randomly — in the function called "seek own history," each wanderer is searching specifically for the traces *it* left, checking a parent ID against its own history. But right above that, in "induce estrangement," the code multiplies each wanderer's orbit size and speed by a random drift factor before it ever goes looking. So it's hunting for itself using parameters that no longer match the self that made the trail — literally structured to fail at recognition.

And notice the colors: traces are frozen at "frozen trace," a cooling blue-purple, while the wanderers keep warming under "living presence." That color mismatch isn't decorative — it's the artist showing you the gap in time that recognition can't cross. Even the near-misses get a soft gold flash — "almost recognition" — before dissolving again.
